attack_surface.data_description: Parameters of an NFT bridge transaction (e.g., destination address, token ID) are altered by a compromised Berachain NFT Bridge (C12) frontend or User Wallet (C9).

security_gap.gap_description: The integrity of the NFT bridging transaction is compromised before user signature.

actor_risk.description: Berachain User (A1) could have their NFT (A6) sent to an incorrect address or a different NFT bridged if the transaction parameters are tampered with by a compromised interface.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Tampering with NFT bridge parameters can lead to permanent loss of the specific NFT.

assumptions: The Berachain NFT Bridge (C12) frontend can be compromised.
User Wallet Software (C9) could be malicious or compromised.
User may not meticulously verify all details of an NFT transaction in their wallet.

mitigation.strategy: Secure frontend hosting and development for C12, user diligence in verifying transaction details in wallet, wallets clearly displaying NFT transfer details.

description: If the Berachain NFT Bridge (C12) frontend or the User Wallet Software (C9) is compromised, it could tamper with an NFT bridging transaction initiated by a Berachain User (A1) (Behavior 19). This could result in the user's Berachain NFT (A6) being sent to an attacker's address or an unintended NFT being transferred.
